calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees;
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Sato, Esq., and Reuben Yeroushalmi, Esq., 310.623.1926, for plaintiff Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc.
Scott Hatch, Esq., 949.717.3000, for defendants Kohl's Corporation, Kohl's Department Stores, Inc. and Kohl's Illinois, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST's Motion For Attorneys Fees is denied. "In order to obtain attorney fees without such a judicially recognized change in the legal relationship between the parties, a plaintiff must establish that: (1) the lawsuit was a catalyst motivating the defendants to provide the primary relief sought; (2) that the lawsuit had merit and achieved its catalytic effect by threat of victory, not by dint of nuisance and threat of expense, as elaborated in Graham; and (3) that the plaintiffs reasonably attempted to settle the litigation prior to filing the lawsuit." (Tipton-Whittingham v. City of Los Angeles (2004) 34 Cal.4th 604, 608.) Plaintiff has not established that the three prongs are met here. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Maria Viscio #12353.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: PETER SATO, ESQ., 310.623.1926, FOR PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC.
SCOTT HATCH, ESQ., 949.717.3000, FOR DEFENDANTS KOHL'S CORPORATION, KOHL'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC. AND KOHL'S ILLINOIS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: DEFENDANTS KOHL'S CORPORATION, KOHL'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC. AND KOHL'S ILLINOIS, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. All requests for judicial notice are granted. The Court has considered both supplemental briefs filed. Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. Res judicata applies, as all three prongs are met. (Lyons v. Security Pacific Nat. Bank (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1001, 1005.) First, there was a final judgment on the merits in John Moore v. Benson Mills, Inc., et al. (Santa Clara Superior Court Case No. 112CV237078). "A court-approved settlement acts as a final judgment on the merits for the purposes of res judicata. A judgment entered ... by consent or stipulation, is as conclusive a ... bar as a judgment rendered after trial." (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694, citations and quotations omitted.) Second, the party against whom res judicata is raised was in privity with the party to the prior adjudication. Citizens acting in the public interest as private attorney generals are in privity with other citizen enforcers of that same right. (Id. at 689-93.) Third, the issues decided in the Moore action are the same as those presented in this action. The Consent Judgment in the Moore action explicitly includes, and amends the Moore complaint to include "vinyl placemats that bear a brand or trademark owned or licensed by Kohl's or affiliated entity that are sold or offered for sale by Kohl's in the State of California." (See Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 3, Consent Judgment, paras. 1.6 "Complaint shall be deemed amended to include all Covered Products as defined in this Consent Judgment'; para. 2.1.1 defining "Covered Products.") The two placemats identified in the Complaint and 60-Day Notices in this action bear a brand or trademark owned or licensed by Kohl's and therefore the claims pertaining to them are released pursuant to Section 6.1 of the Moore Consent Judgment. (Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 3, Consent Judgment, para. 6.1; Defendant's Supplemental Request for Judicial Notice, Exs. 1 and 2.) Because the only two products alleged in the Complaint in this action have been released by the Moore Consent Judgment, there are no remaining allegations in the Complaint. (See generally Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. Kinetsu (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 953.) Additionally, Plaintiff has not established that the narrow public interest exception to res judicata should apply here. (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance(s) on TUE-OCT-01-2013:
Tara Heckard-Bryant, Esq. of Yeroushalmi & Associates [310.623.1926] appearing by telephone for Plaintiff CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC.;
No other appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Plaintiff CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., in the public interest's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Requests For Admission To Defendant Kohl'S Department Stores, Inc. is GRANTED. Defendant Kohl's Department Stores, Inc.'s responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission Nos. 7, 13, 14 and 15 admit the requests for admission for only a portion of the time period stated in the request, without denying or providing a statement of inability to comply with respect to the remaining time period specified in the requests for admission. Requests for Admissions 71 and 72 call for the application of law to fact, and are therefore proper. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Order signed in open Court. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst A. Halperin; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
